commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human/contributor action remains because the branch needs real runtime proof and maintainer acceptance of the text-to-tool recovery boundary after the concrete code blockers are fixed.; Needs attention: The diff changes a security-sensitive execution boundary by promoting provider text into tool calls, and the current patch can execute error-wrapped DSML and retain unbounded provider-controlled text.; Review... commands.review_summary:
The PR captures DeepSeek DSML markup in the text filter, parses recovered invokes into synthetic OpenAI-completions toolCall blocks, skips recovery when native tool calls already exist, and adds parser/filter tests.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +94. Total +204 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main filters DSML text and only executes native choiceDelta.tool_calls, so a response containing only a DSML invoke would not run a tool. No live DeepSeek run was performed in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action remains because the branch needs real runtime proof and maintainer acceptance of the text-to-tool recovery boundary after the concrete code blockers are fixed.

target.title: fix(agents): recover tool calls from DeepSeek DSML text markup
target.author: sebtardif
